name: Current Affairs in Geography, Climate & Disasters (1)

sourceRefs.label: downtoearth.org.in/visionias.in - India's 2025 extreme weather statistics; Climate Risk Index 2025/2026 rankings

researchNotes: Second topic in the new Part A Current-Affairs rotation (Unit II's turn, per the Science→Geography→History→Polity→Economy→TN-Culture cycle). All facts are recent (2025-2026 reports) and explicitly time-stamped — these statistics will be superseded by next year's reports, so the figures should be understood as 'as reported in 2025/2026', not permanent facts. Expansion pass (Batch 12): added Cyclone Ditwah, NDMA institutional structure (Act/hierarchy), 2024-25 hydro-meteorological death toll by state, and the National Disaster Management Plan/Sendai Framework linkage — filling out the syllabus's 'Disaster management' institutional angle alongside the original statistics-only content.

reading.intro: Covers India's recent extreme-weather and climate-disaster statistics, drawn from 2025-2026 climate reports — directly relevant to the syllabus's 'Natural calamity - Disaster management' and 'Climate change' clauses, updated with the latest available figures.

qa.question: How frequently did India face extreme weather events in 2025, per recent reporting?
qa.answer: India faced extreme weather on 331 of 334 days in 2025 — extreme weather events occurred on 99% of days between 1 January and 30 November 2025.

qa.question: What was the human and economic toll of these 2025 extreme weather events?
qa.answer: At least 4,419 deaths; around 17.4 million hectares of cropped land affected; at least 181,459 houses destroyed; and approximately 77,189 animals killed.

qa.question: Where does India rank on the Climate Risk Index 2026, and what are the associated losses?
qa.answer: 9th among the most climate-affected countries, with approximately 80,000 deaths and USD 170 billion in losses attributed to climate impacts.

qa.question: Where did India rank on the Climate Risk Index 2025 (covering the longer period 1993-2022)?
qa.answer: 6th most affected country globally over that period, with losses exceeding USD 180 billion.

qa.question: How has India's average temperature changed in recent decades, and what specific record was set in February 2025?
qa.answer: The Indian landmass was warmer by nearly 0.9°C during 2015-2024 compared to the 1901-1930 baseline. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) confirmed February 2025 as the hottest February on record.

qa.question: What change has been observed in Himalayan glacial lakes, and what risk does this raise?
qa.answer: Glacial lakes and other water bodies in the Himalayan region saw a 10.81% increase in area from 2011 to 2024 due to climate change — raising the risk of catastrophic Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs).

qa.question: Approximately how many people in India have been internally displaced due to climate change, per the IDMC?
qa.answer: About 14 million people, per estimates from the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC).

qa.question: What was Cyclonic Storm Ditwah (Nov-Dec 2025), and what was its toll?
qa.answer: A weak-intensity but catastrophic tropical cyclone that brought heavy rain and flooding to Sri Lanka and Southern India in late November-early December 2025 — killing over 600 people and causing over USD 1.6 billion in damage, mostly in Sri Lanka, illustrating that cyclone intensity alone doesn't determine disaster severity.

qa.question: What is the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA), and who heads it?
qa.answer: India's apex body for disaster management, established under the Disaster Management Act, 2005 — headed by the Prime Minister of India. Below it, State Disaster Management Authorities (SDMA) and District Disaster Management Authorities (DDMA) handle implementation at state and district level.

qa.question: How many deaths occurred due to hydro-meteorological disasters in India in 2024-25, and which states were worst affected?
qa.answer: Over 2,900 deaths — worst-affected states were Himachal Pradesh (408 deaths), Madhya Pradesh (373), Kerala (355), Gujarat (230), and Maharashtra (206).

qa.question: What is the National Disaster Management Plan, and what international framework does it align with?
qa.answer: Released in 2016, it was India's first-ever national plan prepared specifically for disaster management. It aligns India's approach with the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction 2015-2030 (a global UN framework adopted at the 3rd World Conference in Sendai, Japan).

qa.question: What disaster-resilience approach has the Tamil Nadu government adopted for housing reconstruction?
qa.answer: Reconstructing damaged and vulnerable houses using disaster-resistant features, as part of the state's broader post-disaster rehabilitation strategy.

The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) was established under which Act, and is headed by whom?
options: Disaster Management Act 2005, headed by the Home Minister
Disaster Management Act 2005, headed by the Prime Minister
Environment Protection Act 1986, headed by the President
Epidemic Diseases Act 1897, headed by the Prime Minister
questions.explanation: Disaster Management Act 2005, headed by the PM.
questions.trick: Remember the hierarchy top-down: NDMA (national, PM-headed) → SDMA (state) → DDMA (district) — mirrors India's general federal administrative structure.

options: Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction 2015-2030
questions.explanation: Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction 2015-2030.
